section_name: Colorado task force on drunk and impaired driving

subsections.text: The general assembly finds and declares that:

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: Drunk and impaired driving continues to cause needless deaths and injuries, especially among young people;

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: In 2003, there were over thirty thousand arrests for driving under the influence or driving while ability-impaired;

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: Although Colorado has taken many measures to reduce the incidents of drunk and impaired driving, the persistent regularity of these incidents continues to be a problem, as evidenced by the case of Sonja Marie Devries who was killed in 2004 by a drunk driver who had been convicted of drunk driving on six previous occasions; and

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: According to the federal national highway traffic safety administration, other states with a statewide task force on drunk and impaired driving have seen a decrease in incidents of drunk and impaired driving.

There is hereby created the Colorado task force on drunk and impaired driving, referred to in this section as the “task force”. The task force shall meet regularly to investigate methods of reducing the incidents of drunk and impaired driving and develop recommendations for the state of Colorado regarding the enhancement of government services, education, and intervention to prevent drunk and impaired driving.
Intentionally left blank —Ed.

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The task force shall consist of:

subsections.label: I
subsections.text: The executive director of the department of transportation or his or her designee who shall also convene the first meeting of the task force;

subsections.label: II
subsections.text: Three representatives appointed by the executive director of the department of revenue, with the following qualifications:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: One representative with expertise in driver’s license sanctioning;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: One representative with expertise in enforcement of the state’s liquor sales laws; and

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: One representative from the department of revenue’s marijuana enforcement division;

subsections.label: III
subsections.text: The state court administrator or his or her designee;

subsections.label: IV
subsections.text: The chief of the Colorado state patrol or his or her designee;

subsections.label: V
subsections.text: The state public defender or his or her designee;

subsections.label: VI
subsections.text: Two representatives appointed by the commissioner of the behavioral health administration in the department of human services with the following qualifications:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: One representative with expertise in substance abuse education and treatment for DUI or DWAI offenders; and

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: One representative with expertise in providing minors, adolescents, and juvenile offenders with substance abuse treatment and related services;

subsections.label: VII
subsections.text: The director of the division of probation services or his or her designee;

subsections.label: VIII
subsections.text: The executive director of the department of public health and environment, or his or her designee;

subsections.label: IX
subsections.text: The following members selected by the member serving pursuant to subsection (3)(a)(I) of this section:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide association of chiefs of police with experience in making arrests for drunk or impaired driving;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide organization of county sheriffs with experience in making arrests for drunk or impaired driving;

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: A victim or a family member of a victim of drunk or impaired driving;

subsections.label: D
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide organization of victims of drunk or impaired driving;

subsections.label: E
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide organization of district attorneys with experience in prosecuting drunk or impaired driving offenses;

subsections.label: F
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide organization of criminal defense attorneys with experience in defending persons charged with drunk or impaired driving offenses;

subsections.label: G
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide organization that represents persons who sell alcoholic beverages for consumption on premises;

subsections.label: G.5
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide organization that represents persons who sell alcoholic beverages for consumption off premises;

subsections.label: H
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide organization that represents distributors of alcoholic beverages in Colorado;

subsections.label: I
subsections.text: A manufacturer of alcoholic beverages in Colorado;

subsections.label: J
subsections.text: A person under twenty-four years of age who is enrolled in a secondary or postsecondary school;

subsections.label: K
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide organization that represents alcohol and drug addiction counselors;

subsections.label: L
subsections.text: A representative of a statewide organization that represents persons licensed to sell retail marijuana for consumption off premises;

subsections.label: M
subsections.text: A community-based representative of the substance use disorder prevention field; and

subsections.label: N
subsections.text: A representative from the retail or medical marijuana industry who is an owner or manager of a retail dispensary;

subsections.label: X
subsections.text: The director of the peace officers standards and training board or the director’s designee; and

subsections.label: XI
subsections.text: A researcher who is appointed by a majority of the task force members and who specializes in drunk and impaired driving research.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Members selected pursuant to subparagraph (IX) of paragraph (a) of this subsection (3) shall serve terms of two years but may be selected for additional terms.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: Members of the task force shall not be compensated for or reimbursed for their expenses incurred in attending meetings of the task force.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: The initial meeting of the task force shall be convened on or before August 1, 2006, by the member serving pursuant to subparagraph (I) of paragraph (a) of this subsection (3). At the first meeting, the task force shall elect a chair and vice-chair from the members serving pursuant to subparagraphs (I) to (VIII) of paragraph (a) of this subsection (3), who shall serve a term of two years but who may be reelected for additional terms.

subsections.label: e
subsections.text: The task force shall meet not less frequently than bimonthly and may adopt policies and procedures necessary to carry out its duties.
Repealed.
(5)(Deleted by amendment, L. 2011, (SB 11-093), ch. 41, p. 108, § 2, effective March 21, 2011.)
